problem.title: Generate Parentheses
problem.description: Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.
assumptions: Only generate valid combinations (never more close than open at any prefix).

example.explanation: All combinations of 3 pairs of well-formed parentheses.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Backtracking [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Build valid sequences character by character using backtracking, tracking the remaining count of open and close parentheses that can still be placed. An open parenthesis can be added whenever the open count is positive, and a close parenthesis can be added only when more close parentheses remain than open ones, which guarantees the sequence stays well-formed. The key insight is that these two constraints together are both necessary and sufficient for producing all valid combinations.
